title: C1 href masking + C2 shared-DOM param + P5-03 cap + p5-06 regex
acceptance_criteria: C1: in p5-02 and p5-04, before any scanned-site href/URL is placed into finding.evidence or finding.location, strip the query string AND fragment (keep origin+path only). Verify no other p5 module echoes a raw href with a query string; if one does, fix it the same way

C2: add optional `dom?: import('cheerio').CheerioAPI` to ComplianceContext in types.ts (JSDoc: pre-parsed DOM shared by the orchestrator to avoid N re-parses). In every module that calls cheerio.load (p5-01,02,04,05,06), use `const $ = ctx.dom ?? cheerio.load(<existing chosen html>)` — keep the existing cleanedHtml ?? renderedHtml ?? html selection as the fallback. No behaviour change when ctx.dom is absent
Sec Low: in p5-03, cap each raw header value echoed into evidence to a sane max length (mirror the existing C-35 length-cap convention; ~200 chars + ellipsis)
Perf Low: in p5-06, make the auth/rights detection regex(es) safe from catastrophic backtracking (no nested unbounded quantifiers); behaviour unchanged on normal input
All modules remain PURE (no outbound request). `npm run lint` clean (no new errors); `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
